verdicts.reason: Betrothal decision motivates Jaehaerys and Alysanne's free choice to flee and marry in secret; grounded at line 189, tier-1 plain narration.
Marriage motivates Rogar and Alyssa's free decision to travel to Dragonstone with force; grounded at line 215, tier-1.
Marriage is explicitly named 'immediate cause of the tension' (line 35) that festers into Rogar's later urging to depose Jaehaerys; MOTIVATES correctly captures disposition edge.

Quote at line 143 shows Alyssa's motivation (to undermine marriage via household) but does NOT explicitly tie this to the confrontation at gates as trigger. Text shows general plan, not response to failed encounter. Causation is contextually logical but not textually explicit. Recommend: quote text that shows Alyssa's decision as direct response to gates incident, or accept that grounding is implicit/narratively inferred.
Lannisport experience motivates Rhaena's resolve to leave (line 73); later intervening stops and Jaehaerys's free grant separate resolve from actual grant—correct disposition edge with MOTIVATES.
Rogar's proposal (line 159) and Alyssa's dismissal (line 165-167) occur in same unbroken council session; TRIGGERS is correct for next-beat immediacy.
Explicit same-night sequencing: 'On the night of Lord Rogar's dismissal, Ser Ronnal Baratheon and...' (line 181); TRIGGERS is correct.

Rogar's naming of Aerea as alternative monarch motivates Alyssa's preemptive protective action (she 'had stolen a march' before Ronnal could seize her, line 183); MOTIVATES correctly captures her free decision.
Immediate sequence: 'With Lord Rogar gone, Queen Alyssa named her brother Daemon Velaryon as Hand' (line 191); TRIGGERS captures next-beat causation.

Rogar's dismissal and humiliation motivate his desperate final attempt (line 193: 'this matter...had become a war to him...One last folly remained to him'); MOTIVATES correctly captures his free decision to attempt ploy.
Reaching sixteenth nameday (line 167) is legal trigger for majority; immediately followed by return and assumption of rule; TRIGGERS captures this section-break beat.
First act explicitly upon return: 'The young king began his realm by remaking the council' (line 37); TRIGGERS is correct.

Celtigar's failed taxes (narrated earlier, lines 81-89) motivate his dismissal as master of coin on Jaehaerys's first day (line 43); causation clear from narrative sequence and Celtigar's public disfavor.

Quote 'Only when all this had been done to his satisfaction and his new men were in place did Jaehaerys instruct' (line 67) explicitly marks council remaking as satisfied precondition; ENABLES correctly captures this—pardon remains Jaehaerys's free choice.
Orryn's failed ploy and imprisonment break Rogar's will to resist (he 'fell into despair', line 227); this preconditions his receptiveness to the king's summons and mercy—ENABLES is correct.
Consummation (line 153) is direct biological cause of conception; CAUSES is appropriate for mechanical inevitability, not mediated by free decision.

Marriage becoming public motivates Jaehaerys's strategic decision to craft Doctrine of Exceptionalism (line 157: 'With secrecy finally at an end, the king and his court waited...'); MOTIVATES correctly captures his deliberate policy response.

Text explicitly hedges causation ('The why of it is a good deal less certain...surely the Seven Speakers played a part as well', line 185); tier-2/disputed correctly marked—CAUSES is right but with appropriately cautious tier and disputed flag.

Maegor's second marriage (39 AC) is explicitly stated as cause of breach with Faith, which then made Aegon/Rhaena's marriage 'a further outrage' (line 157: 'had shattered...so the marriage...had been seen'). STUB EVENT verified as genuine 39 AC/Sons-of-Dragon reference, grounded retrospectively but explicitly—wired honestly using text actually present in assigned unit.

Rhaena explicitly bundles Aerea request onto Dragonstone grant as second demand (line 302: 'Give me Dragonstone and one thing more'); grant ENABLES the second demand, which remains Jaehaerys's free 'Done.'

Anecdote of Barth passing Mattheus at Bitterbridge (line 61) illustrates council switchover (dismissal/arrival) without causal force; SUB_BEAT_OF correctly captures structural containment within broader remaking event.

Brandon's death enables Walton's succession, giving Walton lordship and thus authority to respond to Night's Watch rebellion (line 97: 'His son Walton succeeded him, and when...he gathered his strength'); ENABLES is correct.

Bracken's beheading occurs within rebellion suppression (line 103: 'the black brothers retook Rimegate...save for Ser Olyver himself, who was beheaded'); PART_OF correctly captures structural containment as component event within rebellion.
Walton's death in rebellion immediately triggers Alaric's succession to lordship (line 109); TRIGGERS captures automatic/immediate succession with no intervening decision.
Franklyn's enforcement of banishment order at the docks directly sparks crowd uprising (lines 53-60: confrontation → crowd anger → overwhelming his men); TRIGGERS is correct for immediate spark.
Celtigar's tax failure creates treasury emergency (line 87: 'building slowed to a halt...trade declined...no longer had funds'); this preconditions decision to seek foreign loans—ENABLES is correct.
Loans directly cause work resumption: 'The securing of the loans had one immediate effect; work on the Dragonpit was able to resume' (line 407); CAUSES correctly captures direct causation.
